metadata.role_title: Machine Learning Engineer / Sr AI Engineer
metadata.match_reason: Zomato + Blinkit jointly hiring AI/ML Engineers to optimize LLMs; Zia AI assistant for restaurants; ML for spam review detection, search ranking, dish tagging.
metadata.notes: Eternal group also includes Blinkit. NLP work on reviews, photos, restaurant search. Python heavy. Weekday: 13.4 LPA @1yr, 21.7 LPA @5yr.

research_summary: Zomato (Eternal) is a 5000+ employee public company running ML at serious scale: search ranking, recommendation engines, LLM-powered customer support (Nugget, 15M+ conversations/month), and NLP for reviews/spam/dish tagging. Expanded OpenAI partnership in Feb 2026. GenAI team grew from 3 to 20+ engineers. Their ML Runtime (Feature Store, Model Store, Model Serving API Gateway) and multi-model approach closely mirrors Amit's production work. Key hook: Amit's FAISS->Milvus migration and BGE-M3 benchmarking is the exact kind of data-driven infra decision their team makes. Agentic RAG with LangGraph also maps to Nugget's architecture.
jd_full_text:
Sr. AI Engineer / Machine Learning Engineer -- Zomato (Eternal)
Location: Gurgaon / Bangalore | Experience: 3-7 years | Salary: 20-34 LPA

About the role:
As an ML Engineer at Zomato, you will be responsible for designing cutting-edge scalable products to meet Zomato's rapidly growing business needs. You will take end-to-end ownership of Machine Learning systems -- from data pipelines and training to real-time prediction engines.

Key responsibilities:
- Maintain and optimize large-scale ML infrastructure that powers Search ranking models
- Build and improve restaurant and dish recommendation engines
- Work with engineers to implement algorithms and systems with strong positive impact on user-facing products
- NLP applications: understanding reviews/comments to extract dish tags, review summaries, spam signals, sentiment analysis
- LLM optimization: fine-tuning, RLHF, scaling AI solutions for food and grocery experiences
- Own end-to-end ML systems: feature engineering, model training, real-time serving
- Work with Kafka, Airflow, Spark, Pulsar for data pipelines
- Contribute to ML Runtime: Feature Compute Engine, Feature Store, Model Store, Model Serving API Gateway

Required skills:
- Strong command of Python (primary) or Go as backend language
- Solid engineering principles, data structures, and algorithms
- Experience with large-scale ML in production
- Familiarity with generative AI, LLM fine-tuning, RLHF, stable diffusion
- NLP expertise: text classification, embeddings, retrieval
- Real-time prediction systems at scale
- Vector databases and embedding models

Nice to have:
- Experience with Kafka/Airflow/Spark/Pulsar
- LangChain / LangGraph / agentic AI systems
- On-premise model hosting (vLLM or similar)
- Multi-model orchestration architectures

About Zomato ML:
Zomato uses ML across: search ranking (neural network with Word2Vec, entity recognition), dish tagging, review mining, photo classification, personalized recommendations, spam detection, delivery logistics optimization, and generative AI (Nugget CX platform, ZIA restaurant assistant). The team runs ML Runtime consisting of Feature Compute Engine, Feature Store, Model Store, and Model Serving API Gateway.
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with production ML + NLP -- JD is ML for search ranking, recommendations, NLP for reviews/dish tags, LLM optimization and fine-tuning. Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques first (NLP, RAG, LLM Orchestration -- JD NLP focus), Languages 2nd, Vector DBs 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.research_hook: Leads recommendation engines and search algorithms at Zomato. Posted hiring for adtech analytics in 2024. Authored or led March 2025 blog on Zomato's ML Runtime (Feature Store, Model Store, Serving API Gateway) -- 4-component ML infrastructure for scalable production ML. IIT Madras. 10K+ followers.
contacts.subject: Zomato ML Runtime and production retrieval
contacts.content:
Hi Naresh,

Came across the March blog on Zomato's ML Runtime -- the breakdown of Feature Store, Model Store, and Serving API Gateway maps almost exactly to a rebuild I did at Silver Touch: separated Llama-2-7b into a VLLM-served API, swapped FAISS for Milvus after benchmarking on production data, and MRR went from 45% to 95% on 50K+ queries/month.

I'm 2 years into production ML: RAG at scale, agentic LangGraph pipelines, multi-model benchmarking across GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and Llama. Been watching what Zomato's team ships on the search ranking and recommendation side. If you're hiring or planning to, I'd like 15 minutes to hear how you think about the retrieval and serving stack.

Open to a call next Tuesday or Thursday?

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Zomato ML Runtime and production retrieval
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Naresh,

Bumping this in case it got buried. One thing I should have mentioned: after the FAISS to Milvus swap at Silver Touch, the bottleneck shifted from retrieval accuracy to query preprocessing latency. Layering an intent classification step before the embedding call cut response time from 16 seconds to 8. Same kind of query preprocessing your scalable ML post touches on with the Feature Compute Engine.

If next week works better for a short call, open to that too.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Zomato ML Runtime and production retrieval
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Naresh,

Last note from me. If Zomato isn't actively hiring on the ML side right now, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Good luck with the adtech analytics build.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: Building Nugget, Zomato's AI-native CX platform that now handles 15M+ conversations/month at 85% resolution. Nugget uses Llama 70B for intent detection + Llama 8B for chat, MongoDB Atlas, policy-layer guardrails, multi-class classification. Launched as enterprise product in 2025. MongoDB case study quote: 'conversations are fragmented, context is constantly evolving, and automation must coexist with human judgment.'
contacts.subject: Nugget's fragmented context problem and LangGraph
contacts.content:
Hi Vivek,

Read the MongoDB case study on Nugget -- your line about conversations being fragmented and context constantly evolving is the exact problem I ran into building a permission-aware RAG chatbot at Vivansh. Our solution was a LangGraph state machine: permission filtering node using a Milvus permission index cross-referenced against PostgreSQL, then content retrieval from 10,000+ chunks, then GPT-4o for answer generation. 500+ concurrent users, 1,000+ queries/day, 250+ permissions enforced per query with no caching possible because two users asking the same question may need different answers.

The parallel to Nugget's intent classification and policy guardrail layer is pretty direct. We also ran Llama on-prem via VLLM for a different client project, so the multi-model routing approach is familiar.

If there's an ML or backend AI role on the Nugget side or the broader Zomato org, I'd like 15 minutes to hear what problems you're currently working on.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Nugget's fragmented context problem and LangGraph
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Vivek,

Following up. One thing I didn't mention: we also built a Selenium crawler that recognized dynamic UI elements (tabs, dropdowns, accordions) to ingest hidden content into our RAG index. Total chunks ended up at 10,000+ across website pages, knowledge base, and course content, with metadata-based filtering at query time. Useful for keeping the index clean without rebuilding constantly -- possibly relevant if Nugget's enterprise expansion means more diverse data sources.

If this week or next works for a call, I'm flexible.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Nugget's fragmented context problem and LangGraph
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Vivek,

Last note. If there's no open role on the Nugget or broader Zomato ML side right now, completely fine. Would still appreciate being on your radar when the team grows.

Good luck with the enterprise rollout.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: ML @ Zomato, Bengaluru. 29K followers. Active posts on LoRA fine-tuning (attention-only vs MLP-only LoRA efficiency) and ML engineering interviews. Posts show interest in practical LLM optimization and empirical testing -- exactly Zomato's LLM mandate. Ex-Google AI Consultant.
contacts.subject: LoRA fine-tuning post and empirical model testing
contacts.content:
Hi Anshuman,

Saw your posts on LoRA fine-tuning -- specifically the point about attention-only LoRA leaving performance on the table compared to MLP-only matching all-layer on fewer params. That data-driven framing is the same mindset I used when selecting models for a form generation pipeline: GPT-4o produced reasonable blueprints but Qwen3-Coder-480B beat it on structural accuracy after testing both across 50+ form templates. Empirical selection over just using the biggest available model.

I'm 2 years into production ML: RAG at 50K+ queries/month, agentic LangGraph pipelines at Vivansh, on-prem vLLM serving at Silver Touch. Following what Zomato's team is shipping on the LLM optimization side. If your team is hiring or you know who is, I'd appreciate a pointer.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: LoRA fine-tuning post and empirical model testing
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anshuman,

Following up. One more data point from the production side: after picking BGE-M3 over 5 other embedding models (BGE small/base/large, Stella, SPLADE) based on MRR on actual production data, the next bottleneck was reranking latency. Ended up adding a Cohere reranker in the video course pipeline and it improved selection quality noticeably. Similar kind of two-stage evaluation you'd run when comparing LoRA configurations.

If you could point me to who handles ML hiring at Zomato, that'd be helpful too.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: LoRA fine-tuning post and empirical model testing
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Anshuman,

Last note from me. If now's not the right time or there's no open spot, totally fine. Happy to stay on your radar.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: Data Scientist at Zomato, IIT Delhi. Connected to WeatherUnion (Zomato's hyperlocal weather service). Amit also did research at IIT Delhi (Dr. Lalan Kumar, Dept of EE) -- shared institutional ground and data-driven ML research background.
contacts.subject: IIT Delhi connection and data science at Zomato
contacts.content:
Hi Anish,

Noticed you're at Zomato and did your academic work at IIT Delhi -- I was there too, research intern in the EE department under Dr. Lalan Kumar from Jan to May 2024. Small world. (Evaluated 1,000+ model configurations for a wearable gesture recognition system, so the experimental rigor side of IIT Delhi research.)

I'm now 2 years into production ML: rebuilt a RAG system from 45% to 95% MRR on 50K+ queries/month, benchmarked 6 embedding models before picking BGE-M3, built a LangGraph agentic chatbot at Vivansh enforcing 250+ per-user permissions. The NLP and recommendation work at Zomato is exactly the direction I want to go.

If there's an open ML or data science role on your side of the org, or you know who's hiring, I'd appreciate a pointer. Happy to share my work too.

Amit

Following up briefly. Also noticed you're working on WeatherUnion -- hyperlocal weather data is a genuinely hard problem (sensor density, interpolation, micro-climate variation). The data engineering side of that must be interesting.

If there's a good moment to chat about the Zomato ML side more broadly, I'm around. Or happy to just stay on your radar for when something opens up.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Data Science at District by Zomato (premium dining/experiences platform, Gurgaon). NIT Agartala. Working on DS problems for District's recommendation and personalization stack. District is part of Eternal alongside Blinkit and Zomato.
contacts.subject: District's recommendation problems and my ML background
contacts.content:
Hi Anukul,

Was reading about Eternal's product split -- Zomato for food delivery, Blinkit for quick commerce, District for premium dining. The personalization and recommendation problems across those three are pretty different from each other. Curious about what District's DS stack looks like specifically.

Quick background: 2 years production ML, built retrieval and recommendation systems at scale (50K+ queries/month, 2-stage RAG with per-user filtering across 750+ content items at Vivansh, Milvus vector DB benchmarked against FAISS and Qdrant on real production data). Most recently at Vivansh building agentic LangGraph pipelines for an enterprise platform.

If District's data science team is hiring or expanding, I'd appreciate knowing. Or if you could point me to who handles ML recruiting across the Eternal group, that works too.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: District's recommendation problems and my ML background
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anukul,

Following up. One thing worth adding: I also built a video course generation pipeline where RAG was used to match 60-second lesson segments from a library of 10,000+ pre-processed clips (Cohere embed + Milvus + Gemini 2.5 Pro for visual-audio alignment). The core challenge was relevance over a diverse multi-modal corpus, which probably has some overlap with what District does for curating dining experiences or matching users to venues.

If there's a good time for a short call, I'm flexible.

Amit
